intro: Rathbunia pseudosonorensis, also known as Devil's Claw, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The root of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antipyretic properties, providing relief from pain and fever, as well as soothing digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Rathbunia pseudosonorensis include iridoid glycosides, specifically harpagoside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Devil's Claw include capsules, tablets, and teas, often used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other inflammatory conditions.
